creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
I had checked the bug fix patch as promised, but was annoyed by the fast that it did not follow the (communicated) requirement of commits to the project. I had run out of time, that weekend, when breaking it into diffs that address just only one aspect each, as is required for commits to the FreeBSD base system. We accept large/complex patch sets for contributed software that did not originate on BSD (since we cannot follow individual cahanges), but the calendar program shall only receive commits that address one bug or feature at a time. And each such commit needs to be accompanied by documentation that not only describes the change but gives a rationale for non-obvious changes.

My issues with the patch file started with the initial chunk that introduces ```f_time_set```. This is a functional change that needs to be documented, and I do not see why it is required (it has not been for more than 30 years). An invalid time will make mktime return -1 and due to the removal of a test of ```f_time <= 0``` this case will not be detected and handled (the current code ignores the invalid date option, the patched code uses an unexpected constant).

There is no documentation (of the patches in the file) to work with and it takes a lot of effort to split the large patch file according to the changed functionality for individual commits and to write functional descriptions of each such commit. And then there should be test cases that fail on the unpatched version and succeed on the patched version.
